Our landscape laborers perform the following duties.  In the early part of the season, they perform clean-up work with rakes, chain saws, tarps, and shovels; they spread mulch using shovels, rakes, wheel barrows, and blowers.  When the weather is warm enough, they plant trees, shrubs, and flowers and water them in using shovels, picks, rakes, wheel barrows, hoses, and watering cans.  Then, when the grass starts to grow, they mow and trim grass using mowers, line trimmers, edgers, and blowers.  Throughout the growing season, they will lay sod, haul topsoil, smooth out uneven terrain, trim trees and bushes, clean up leaves and other debris, fertilize lawns, and pull weeds, using rakes, wheel barrows, rototillers, pruners, shovels, and other hand tools.  As the season comes to a close, the mowing slows down and the work shifts to Fall clean ups which consists mostly of raking leaves and other debris.  These duties are performed for both our residential and commercial clients.
Move soil, equipment, materials, dig holes for plants, trees; install landscape materials & sprinkler systems; weed, spread mulch, clean up, mow, trim. Landscape general activities.

Soft Serve Ice Cream: To make the ice cream, the mix is poured into the Spaceman 6210 Soft Serve Ice Cream Machine, it is connected to electricity and water, and we get the ice cream. The toppings are in small bins at the front edge of the counter; with scoops we scoop the toppings onto the ice cream. Mix the ice-cream using whatever ingredients the customer wants, using a spatula. The spatula is also used to scrape the mixed ice cream off the counter and into a bowl or waffle cone for serving. 
Corn Dogs: Corn dogs frozen, pre-battered and pre-skewered. Corn Dogs are thawed out. Once thawed out they are placed into the deep fryer. The timer for the deep fryer that is reserved for the corn dogs is pre-set, and when they are in the fryer for the proper amount of time the basket raises to allow for the oil to drain from the corn dogs. They are placed into a chafing dish on the front counter under a heat lamp. When a client orders one or more corn dogs, they are removed from the chafing dish and placed into the proper size serving boat. They are handed to the client who would go to the kiosk to add condiments and pick up any napkins or cutlery required. 
Fountain Soda. These workers would NOT be serving any alcoholic beverages. For a fountain drink, the worker would take the proper size cup in their hand, use an ice scoop to scoop ice into the cup (unless the patron ordered no ice), place the cup under the nozzle, press the cup against the lever and fill the cup. Take a lid from the lid supply and secure this on the top of the cup. From time to time, the worker would need to restock the cups, lids and ice from the appropriate storage area. If the machine runs out of syrup, the worker would need to locate in the storage area the proper flavor of prepackaged syrup, disconnect the empty syrup container, connect the full syrup container, and then discard the empty container. 
Lemonade: On a cutting board, the lemons would be cut in half. See through plastic cups and lids would be brought from storage. A food service quality bin of ice with ice scoop would be placed into the work area. Workers serving lemonade would stand at the front counter, communicating with the customer. After taking the order, would scoop ice into a cup, pick up (with gloved hand), and squeeze Lemon Juice into the cup, using a Squeezer. Pre-mixed sugar water would be poured into the cup, and then the product would be served with lid and straw to the patron.

Job requires worker to prepare trawler for fishing activities; put nets into water and retrieve them; sort and head shrimp catch; return undesirable and illegal catch to sea; place shrimp on baskets, rinse, dip in preservatives; assist rigger to ice down shrimp in boat's hold, get nets back into water to resume trawling, and retrieve them again; offload catch, mop deck, assist captain and rigger as required. -Set the forms that hold concrete to the desired pitch and depth, and align them. 
-Spread, level, and smooth concrete, using rake, shovel, hand or power trowel, hand or power screed,

Lemonade, In a cutting board and knife, the lemons would be cut in half. See through plastic cups and lids would be brought from storage. A food service quality bin of ice with ice scoop would be placed into the work area. Workers serving lemonade would stand at the front counter, communicating with the customer. After taking the order, would scoop ice into a cup, pick up (with gloved hand), and squeeze Lemon Juice into the cup, using a Squeezer. Pre-mixed sugar water would be poured into the cup, and then the product would be served with lid and straw to the patron after payment. 
Funnel Cakes, Corn Dogs. Items would be cooked in deep fryers. The workers would stand in front of the specific unit they oversaw operating. On one side of the unit there would be the raw materials (such as skewered hot dogs, corn dog, funnel cake & Oreo batter). A portion of the batter would be taken from the container mixed in and scooped using a large ladle into the funnel cake pourer. The batter would be poured slowly into the basket, in one general location, so that strings of batter would cling together. When one side of the funnel cake is brown, the worker would use a large spoon (to drain grease) to flip the half-cooked funnel cake. When the funnel cake is fully cooked its placed into a tray to allow the excess grease to drain while keeping the funnel cake at a proper serving temperature. 
Same process is done for corn dogs.

RFI response: The worker performs routine fast food service and support duties in both a commercial kitchen and at mobile food carts. The position includes taking customer orders, handling transactions, serving prepared meals, and assisting with cleaning and basic food-related tasks. Work is performed at assigned mobile food cart locations and the company’s commercial kitchen facility.
At the mobile food cart, the worker sets up the cart at the beginning of the shift, stocks supplies, wipes down surfaces, and prepares the service area. Throughout the day, the worker greets customers, takes food orders, and operates a basic point-of-sale device to process transactions (typically credit/debit cards). Meals are handed directly to the customer once assembled. The worker ensures the front-of-house area is kept neat and sanitary, replaces utensils and packaging as needed, and maintains a polite and helpful customer service presence at all times.
At the commercial kitchen, the worker supports food service operations by performing basic cleaning and preparation tasks. The worker may wash and sort vegetables, portion sauces, and carry trays to and from holding areas. No advanced cooking or recipe development is required.
Daily responsibilities include restocking supplies and ingredients, assisting with the setup and breakdown of food service areas, and performing cleaning tasks such as wiping counters, washing utensils by hand, removing trash, and sweeping or mopping floors. The worker washes all pots, pans, and food service equipment used during the shift and ensures that all items are stored in a clean and orderly condition. Mobile carts and related food service equipment are washed, sanitized, and maintained according to health and safety standards.
The company operates a licensed commercial kitchen, which handles the preparation and packaging of food, and a fleet of mobile food carts where final food service and customer interaction occurs. The kitchen is used for batch-cooking and assembly, while carts are used to reheat, grill, and serve food to the public. Workers may rotate between the kitchen and mobile carts based on scheduling needs.
Equipment and tools used in this role include: basic point-of-sale devices, grills, warmers, utensils, knives, cutting boards, pans, pots, food trays, ladles, tongs, cleaning cloths, sanitation spray, gloves, trash bags, soap, and mop buckets. Workers are expected to follow clear instructions and food safety procedures at all times.
Tasks are repetitive, clearly defined, and suited for entry-level fast food service workers. The worker does not engage in independent food planning or supervise others as a standard function of the job.

Funnel Cakes: Gold Medal Products1 sells the mix that is used. Following instructions printed on the mix container, pour the mix plus appropriate liquid into the commercial batter blender. Once the batter has been blended, a portion is scooped into a dispenser bottle. The deep fryer would contain cooking oil at the proper temp. Worker would slowly pour batter from dispenser bottle into the fryer so that strings of batter would cling together, forming a funnel cake. When one side of the funnel cake is brown, the worker would use a large, slotted spoon to flip the half cooked funnel cake. When the funnel cake is fully cooked the worker would use the same large, slotted spoon to pick the funnel cake from the fryer, and place into a chafing dish under heat lamp. After customer places order, worker would use tongs to place funnel cake into serving boat and add selected topping such as sugar, shipped cream, compote, chocolate sauce and/or Nutella ®, to the funnel cake and then provide the product to the customer. 
Deep Fried Oreos: The same batter that was prepared for funnel cakes (above) would be used for deep fried Oreos ®. Worker would pour batter into a small chafing dish. Oreos would be picked up by gloved hand, dropped into the batter to be coated, and then dropped into the hot oil of the deep fryer. They would float to the surface, be scooped up with slotted spoon, and placed into a small chafing dish under heat lamp. After customer places order, worker would use tongs to place Fried Oreos into serving boat and add selected topping to the Oreos and then provide the product to the customer.
Fountain Soda. Lemonade, On a cutting board, the lemons would be cut in half. See through plastic cups and lids would be brought from storage. Bin of ice with ice scoop would be placed into the work area. Workers serving lemonade would stand at the front counter. After taking order, scoop ice into a cup, and squeeze Lemon Juice into the cup, using a Squeezer. Pre-mixed sugar water would be poured into the cup, and then the product would be served with lid and straw to the patron after payment.

The structural members are for construction and structure framework. Iron being worked could be for main rack, vendor steel , or perimeter structures around job blueprint. 
lronworkers can be involved in the operation of manual or hydraulic hoisting equipment's if training has been given or if a competent person (Rigger) is overseeing the rigging activity. 

But as for rigging structural members for erection from a crane hook , lronworkers do not perform that activity . lronworkers can help clear travel paths or possibly hold a tag line . If the Ironworker is a connector, they might be involved in safely removing rigging hardware after steel member has been installed . 
Responsibilities would be operating hoisting device as directed by (team) directions , typically being a qualified rigger with proper training . Tending with operation might/will include assisting with installing hoisting hardware. Etc. operating elevating work platform to work location or helping move hoisting equipment on location. Any craft member can operate a hoisting device if they have gone through the proper training, but would only be considered assisting and not directing the hoisting operation. 

lronworkers do not operate any crane or rigging when steel erection is being done with a crane. The rigging department handles that as a whole. lronworkers can assist by moving steel with forklift assistance for crane setup or notifying the riggers what pieces will be installed & in which order. lronworkers will also be able to tend as in clearing flight paths for steel erection or holding taglines. More likely than not riggers on a crane work in pairs so no rigging hardware will need to be handled by lronworkers.

The position of the Recreational Leader is of vital importance to Aquasafe's economic vitality. Recreational Leaders will be assigned to specific recreation facilities managed by our company. During the spring, Recreational Leaders will assist our staff in preparing recreational facilities for inspection by the Health Department. They will inspect and maintain the cleanliness of the pool. They use chemicals and equipment to clean pools, furniture, and decks, and otherwise prepare the grounds and facilities in contracted properties. Workers will examine areas for any safety hazards or damage to facilities or equipment, correct settings and perform routine maintenance activities to the pool, pool equipment and decks. They will maintain the quality of the pool water by testing chemical levels and by adding pool water balancing chemicals. Once the facilities are open to the public in the summer months, Recreational Leaders will continue to inspect the cleanliness and maintain the pools' water quality. In addition, they will be responsible for providing leadership in the recreational facilities managed by Aquasafe by patrolling and monitoring swimming areas and enforcing facilities' rules. Recreational Leaders will observe the activities of swimming facilities to prevent and detect hazards and will promote the safety of the pools and surrounding areas. They will use specialized knowledge and skills in CPR, First Aid, personal safety, and swimming instructions to ensure the safety of patrons. Recreational Leaders might assist Recreational Workers in promoting safety in the facilities and perform additional related duties as assigned. Workers must demonstrate the ability to swim and stay under water for short periods. Workers must be able to follow directions and perform basic manual labor. No experience is required. Workers must possess a CPR and First Aid certification. Must either possess a valid Red Cross Lifeguard Certification or obtain upon hiring.
